scope: TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 Flash 4-layer MORI+AITER routed padded VJP backward with route-flattened AsyncLL dX/dTopk return.

source_change.contract: Expand learned-router [T, K] routes into [T*K, 1] before backward MORI dispatch so source rows identify selected routes uniquely.
source_change.op_shape_change: Flattened backward x/grad/topk MORI ops use top_k=1 and max_tokens=T*K; retained sync and asyncll_staged contracts are unchanged.

why: Normal MORI dispatch source-position metadata identifies source input rows, not selected top-k route slots.
At S4096, learned routing can send multiple slots from one token to the same destination rank, so independent AsyncLL handles cannot be aligned safely by src_pos or by reconstructed route_key.
The prior standalone route-flatten probe showed [T*K, 1] dispatch removes duplicate source rows; this artifact validates the same idea inside TorchTitan autograd.

runs.shape: 4xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP4 EP4 MBS1 GBS4 S128
runs.read: Integrated smoke only; throughput is not meaningful at this one-step short shape.

runs.shape: 4xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP4 EP4 MBS1 GBS4 S128
runs.read: Same seed and shape sync reference; independent launches are not bit-exact in loss, but grad_norm matches.

runs.shape: 4xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP4 EP4 MBS1 GBS4 S4096
runs.failure: MORI static heap exhausted before route alignment was tested
runs.read: Failure is capacity from extra flattened AsyncLL buffers, not the previous source-position or route-key mismatch.

runs.shape: 4xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP4 EP4 MBS1 GBS4 S4096
runs.read: Long-sequence route-flattened training gate passes. This proves the flattened route identity contract, not performance.

runs.shape: 4xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP4 EP4 MBS1 GBS4 S4096
runs.read: Seeded sync context run. The route-flattened diagnostic is slower because it duplicates routes and uses larger AsyncLL buffers.

conclusion.route_identity: The [T*K, 1] contract fixes the S4096 independent-handle route identity issue well enough to complete TorchTitan backward.
conclusion.performance: Do not promote asyncll_flattened as a retained performance path; it needs 96G MORI heap at S4096 GBS4 and is slower than sync in this diagnostic shape.
conclusion.next_step: Use this as evidence for the production overlap design: carry explicit per-route identity or shared MORI route/order metadata without materializing duplicated [T*K, H] x/grad dispatches, then retry dX return overlap.
